top_categories.workout_context: 345 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
At Peaks and Plains, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
23,950ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
69ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 345 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
345 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Peaks and Plains. Train it until it feels boring.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 23,950ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Peaks and Plains' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
Low-cadence force work for 23,950ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
23,950ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Peaks and Plains' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 345 miles.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 345 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 345 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Peaks and Plains. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Over Under, and Mixed Climbing to prepare you for 345 miles of mixed terrain in Magdeburg/Harz Mountains, Germany.
